metadata.source: SPE/OnePetro/JPT papers and related references for NCS reservoir fluid characterization
search_queries: SPE paper Norwegian Continental Shelf reservoir fluid characterization
SPE oil viscosity North Sea reservoir Ekofisk Statfjord
SPE paper Troll field reservoir oil viscosity API gravity PVT
SPE paper Gullfaks Oseberg reservoir fluid PVT properties
SPE paper Johan Sverdrup reservoir oil properties API gravity GOR
SPE paper Valhall Heidrun reservoir fluid properties density viscosity
Snorre/Heidrun SPE reservoir oil API gravity bubble point
onepetro.org NCS reservoir fluid Norwegian Continental Shelf PVT viscosity correlation
Breidablikk SPE reservoir oil viscosity API NCS field
metadata.notes: Many full SPE papers are paywalled; extracted values come from abstracts, JPT summary articles, and freely available secondary references. Where a paper exists but no values were exposed in the abstract, the paper is listed as a reference with paper_url but null fluid fields.

papers.title: An Improved Correlation Approach to Predict Viscosity of Crude Oil Systems on the NCS
papers.notes: NCS-wide PVT database. Reports temperatures below 190 F and low/high API gravity categories but does not disclose field-level numbers (proprietary).

papers.title: Holistic Evaluation of Reservoir Oil Viscosity in Breidablikk Field - Including Mud Gas Logging Approach
papers.notes: Two reservoir fluid samples from exploration wells, viscosities of 4 cp and 8 cp. Methane/propane ratio identified as best correlated parameter for reservoir oil viscosity. 20-30% recovery sensitivity to viscosity uncertainty.

papers.title: Evaluation of PVT Comparisons and GOR Prediction Based on Advanced Mud Gas Data: A Case Study from Snorre Field
papers.notes: Cited reference values: API ~44, viscosity 0.86 cP, reservoir T ~100 C, formation T ~90 C with reservoir pressure >300 bar. Reservoir at 2000-2700 m depth, Statfjord/Lunde/Alke formations.

papers.title: Reservoir Characterization of Ekofisk Field: A Giant, Fractured Chalk Reservoir in the Norwegian North Sea
papers.notes: Discovered 1969. Initial reservoir pressure 7120-7135 psia at 10400 ft TVDSS, bubble point ~1600 psi below initial pressure. High-porosity (~30-40%) fractured chalk in Ekofisk and Tor formations, matrix perm ~1 mD, effective perm 1-50 mD.

papers.title: Reservoir Management of the Gullfaks Main Field
papers.notes: Brent Group (Middle Jurassic), Statfjord Group, Cook and Lunde formations. Reservoir at 1700-2000 m. Some areas contain high H2S. Stock tank oil ~860 kg/m3 ~ 33 API.

papers.title: Recovering More Than 70% From the Johan Sverdrup Field / Toward a Recovery Ambition of More Than 70% for the Johan Sverdrup Field
papers.notes: Hydrostatic pressure, undersaturated oil with low GOR (~40 Sm3/Sm3). Non-biodegraded, heavily undersaturated. Multi-darcy shallow marine sandstone (Upper Jurassic).

papers.title: Heidrun field fluid characterization (Equinor / open references)
papers.venue: Field development docs / Wikipedia / industry refs
papers.notes: Naphthenic crude. Lower/Middle Jurassic sandstone in Are, Tilje, Ile, Garn formations. Gas cap above oil. Haltenbanken, Norwegian Sea, water depth 350 m.

papers.title: The Valhall Field: a geological overview
papers.notes: Paraffinic oil 36 API. Upper Cretaceous chalk (Tor and Hod formations). Very high primary porosity preserved by overpressure. Low matrix permeability.

papers.title: The Statfjord Field, Blocks 33/9, 33/12 Norwegian sector, Blocks 211/24, 211/25 UK sector, Northern North Sea
papers.notes: Light, low-sulfur crude. Undersaturated, no natural gas cap. High FVF (2.7 Rbbl/STB) indicates relatively high gas in solution. Brent and Statfjord Group Jurassic sandstones.

papers.title: Dynamic Reservoir Characterization and Production Optimization by Integrating Intelligent Inflow Tracers and Pressure Transient Analysis in a Long Horizontal Well for the Ekofisk Field
papers.notes: Paper reference; no fluid values exposed in abstract.

papers.title: Focused Sampling of Reservoir Fluids Achieves Undetectable Levels of Contamination
papers.venue: SPE Asia Pacific Oil and Gas Conference 2006
papers.notes: Methodology paper on focused-probe sampling for clean PVT samples on NCS; no field-level values disclosed.

papers.title: Fluid Profiling for Reservoir Evaluation: Two Norwegian Case Studies
papers.notes: DFA (downhole fluid analysis) case studies. Field names not disclosed; values not in abstract.

papers.title: Uncertainties in Reservoir Fluid Description for Reservoir Modeling
papers.notes: Discusses lab analysis, sampling uncertainty, process description, and intra-reservoir variation for NCS Statoil-operated fields (would include Statfjord, Gullfaks, Heidrun, Snorre, Troll). No specific field values in abstract.

papers.title: Interpretation of Permanent Well Monitoring Data to Improve Characterization of a Giant Oil Field
papers.notes: Permanent monitoring/characterization paper. No fluid values exposed in abstract.

Troll.notes: No specific oil API/GOR/bubble point recovered from SPE-indexed open sources. Mostly gas with thin oil rim.

Oseberg.notes: No SPE-paper-level open API/GOR/bubble point data recovered. Brent Group reservoir (Oseberg, Tarbert, Etive, Ness formations).
